title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the reversed list.
hints: Think about what happens to each node's next pointer when you reverse.
You need three pointers: previous, current, and next. At each step, redirect current's next to previous.
Initialize prev = null, curr = head. Save curr.next, point curr.next to prev, advance prev and curr.
follow_ups: How would you reverse only a portion of the list?

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following next.

examples.input: head = [3,2,0,-4] with tail connecting to node index 1

hints: If you keep traversing a list with a cycle, you'll never reach null. But how do you detect this efficiently?
Use two pointers moving at different speeds — a slow pointer and a fast pointer.
Slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2 steps. If there's a cycle, fast will eventually catch up to slow.
follow_ups: Can you find where the cycle begins?
Can you prove why fast and slow meet?

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hints: Think about how you'd merge two sorted arrays — same idea applies here.
Use a dummy head node. Compare the current nodes of both lists and attach the smaller one.
Create a dummy node. While both lists have nodes, compare and link the smaller. Attach the remaining list at the end.
follow_ups: How would you merge k sorted lists?

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the Least Recently Used (LRU) cache constraints. Implement get(key) and put(key, value) operations, both in O(1) time. When the cache reaches capacity, evict the least recently used item.

examples.output: get returns values, -1 if not found

hints: You need O(1) lookup and O(1) removal/insertion for ordering. What two data structures together give you this?
A hash map for O(1) lookup combined with a doubly linked list for O(1) ordering operations.
Hash map maps key to DLL node. On access, move node to front. On eviction, remove from tail. On put, add to front.
follow_ups: How would you make this thread-safe?
